name: Chinese Popcorn (Sweet & Pungent Shrimp)
recipeInstructions:
------------------SWEET AND PUNGENT SAUCE------------------
4 ½ tbsp Sugar
4 ½ tbsp Catsup
¼ cup Vinegar
½ tsp Salt
½ tbsp Sherry
½ tsp Cornstarch
1 tbsp Oil
2 large Garlic cloves, minced
¾ tbsp Minced fresh ginger root
1 tbsp Chopped green onion
½ tsp Crushed red pepper
½ tsp Lemon zest
½ tsp Orange zest
1. Peel and devein the shrimp. Slice them lengthwise.
2. Rinse well and pat dry. Mix shrimp with beaten egg white, then coat with a mixture of cornstarch and salt.
3. Coat the shrimp again with oil, ensuring they are thoroughly coated.
4. Refrigerate at least 2 hours to allow flavors to meld.
5. Remove and dust shrimp with remaining cornstarch; ensure they are dry to the touch.
6. Heat oil in a large wok to 350-375°F (175-190°C). Fry shrimp for 1½–2 minutes, separating them carefully with a wooden spoon or chopsticks to avoid sticking. Fry in batches if necessary.
7. Remove shrimp and drain well on paper towels.
8. Combine shrimp with sweet and pungent sauce and toss quickly to coat evenly.
9. Transfer to a platter and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
SWEET AND PUNGENT SAUCE:
1. In a separate bowl, combine sugar, catsup, vinegar, and salt.
2. Heat oil in the wok until hot.
3. Add garlic, ginger, green onion, red pepper, lemon zest, and orange zest; cook for 30 seconds.
4. Stir in the sugar-catsup mixture.
5. Immediately add sherry mixture and cook until slightly thickened.
